title: provider-exceptions.v1 — the committed ledger of sanctioned runtime-parity exceptions
description: The whole-file contract for the provider-exception ledger (.engine/policy/provider-exceptions.json) — the single home for every deliberate difference between what the engine exposes on one AI runtime and the other (eADR-0035). The parity check compares the two adapters capability-by-capability in both directions and consults this ledger for the sanctioned differences; an asymmetry with no entry here is a hard finding. An entry declares not just absence but WEAKNESS: `missing_or_weaker: missing` records a capability with no counterpart, `weaker` records a counterpart that exists in a reduced form (an instruction where the other runtime has mechanical enforcement, a protection the platform can switch off). Every entry carries a plain-language reason and the decision record that sanctions it. The record shape is CLOSED (additionalProperties: false) because the engine wholly owns this file — an unrecognized field is a mistake, not platform growth. The ledger file sits inside the guardrail-weakening floor, so widening it is held for the operator's deliberate acknowledgment at merge; this schema is enforced live by the provider-exceptions-schema check (a validators-core schema rule) via an explicit rule-level schema override.

$schema.description: Optional pointer from the ledger data file to this governing schema, for editor and tooling validation.

exceptions.description: The sanctioned differences, one record each. An empty list is legal (full parity, nothing sanctioned).

kind.description: Which capability family the exception belongs to — the same families the parity check compares (for example: skill, hook, agent, mcp, floor, enforcement). A well-formed string; the parity check owns the family vocabulary so it can grow with the comparison, never this schema.

id.description: The specific capability the exception covers, named the way the parity check names it (a skill verb, a hook event/script pair, a persona slug, a server name, or a described enforcement property).

missing_or_weaker.description: missing = the runtime has no counterpart at all; weaker = a counterpart exists but in a reduced form, and the reason says how.

provider.description: The runtime the exception applies to — the side that is missing the capability or carries the weaker form.

reason.description: The plain-language why: what makes this difference deliberate or platform-inherent, written for the operator reading the ledger at a merge.

contract_ref.description: The decision record that sanctions this exception (an eADR id, for example eADR-0035).
